model: claude-fable-5 (session extraction; reproduce with run_extraction.py --extractor llm)
note: Faithful extraction from each drug's OWN openFDA label text. Empty edge lists are HONEST misses where the label does not self-attribute a CYP3A4 role (a source-coverage gap, not an extraction error). strength is emitted only when the label uses a strength adjective (strong/potent/moderate/weak); otherwise null — surfacing that labels frequently omit graded strength.

edges.evidence_sentence: Simvastatin is a substrate of CYP3A4 and of the transport protein OATP1B1.
Lovastatin is metabolized by CYP3A4 but has no CYP3A4 inhibitory activity.
Atorvastatin is a substrate of CYP3A4 and transporters.
Concomitant administration of clarithromycin with statins that are extensively metabolized by CYP3A4 (lovastatin or simvastatin) is contraindicated.
Itraconazole and its major metabolite, hydroxy-itraconazole, are potent CYP3A4 inhibitors.
In vitro studies have shown that CYP3A4 is the major enzyme involved in the metabolism of itraconazole.
Diltiazem is an inhibitor of CYP3A4 and has been shown to increase significantly the AUC of some statins.
Lower starting and maintenance doses of other CYP3A4 substrates (e.g., atorvastatin) may be required as verapamil may increase the plasma concentration of these drugs.
In vitro metabolic studies indicate that verapamil is metabolized by cytochrome P450 CYP3A4, CYP1A2, CYP2C8, CYP2C9, and CYP2C18.
Lower starting dose of other CYP3A4 substrates may be required.
Avoid the use of rifampin, a strong CYP3A4 inducer, if possible.
The use of concomitant strong CYP3A4 inducers such as carbamazepine should be avoided with temsirolimus.
CYP3A4 inhibitors inhibit carbamazepine metabolism and can thus increase plasma carbamazepine levels.
Coadministration of other drugs metabolized via CYP3A4 such as erythromycin, pimozide, and quinidine are contraindicated in patients receiving fluconazole.
These effects appear to be due to the inhibition of CYP3A4 by nefazodone.
